page: Amazon Web Services r8id.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is bundled with 5700 GB of NVMe SSD instance store capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a 40 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier status in Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Passmark database operations. Memory bandwidth tests show top-tier performance for read-only small and large blocks, alongside strong write performance for large blocks. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Redis SET operations also rank in the top tier, while single-core stress-ng performance remains in the average tier. The processor's support for Advanced Matrix Extensions contributes to top-tier prompt processing speeds for medium and large language models, though text generation speeds for large models fall into the average tier.

Given the high density of DDR5 memory and the inclusion of 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, the r8id.24xlarge offers high resource density for memory-bound and I/O-intensive workloads. This hardware profile is well-suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data analytics, and memory-intensive enterprise applications. The lack of a GPU makes it less suitable for heavy model training, but its CPU-based matrix acceleration supports efficient inference workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services r8id.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, multi-core CPU benchmarks, and database operations. The instance is highly optimized for memory-intensive workloads, showing top-tier results in Redis SET operations and multi-threaded decompression. While single-core performance is average, its support for Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions enables strong performance in CPU-based LLM prompt processing. This server is designed for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5700 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5700 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring fast DDR5 RAM, massive local NVMe storage, and advanced matrix acceleration for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation performance
Intel AMX support for accelerated CPU-based inference
Designed for large databases, Redis caching, and data analytics
